The Fat Pig Society Partnership

Village Socialite was built to curate the best of the best for our neighbors not just products with pretty labels, but brands with Heritage Standards. We chose the Fat Pig Society because they are the real deal. They grow, process, and package their own organic hemp on-site in Colorado. No middlemen, no mystery ingredients just honest, farmer-direct medicine that we are proud to stand behind.

The Economics

Farmer-Direct
Integrity

By operating as a volunteer-driven cooperative, they ensure 80% of every dollar stays on the farm. While most brands prioritize stockholders, this model prioritizes the purity of the plant and the health of the soil.

The Objective

Keeping Family
Farms Alive

A CSU study identified $100,000 in annual revenue as the survival point for small organic farms. This partnership helps Colorado farmers hit that “magic number,” protecting their land from developers for the next generation.

The Name

Proof Over
Promises

The name comes from a founding lesson: prove the model through undeniable results. Whether it’s raising a 250lb pig in Micronesia or producing the cleanest CBD on the market, they believe in results you can see and feel.

The Fat Pig Society

A Worker-Owned Cooperative • Fort Collins, CO

Our Partnership Philosophy

At Village Socialite, we value transparency above all else. The Fat Pig Society isn’t just a supplier; they are a collective of volunteers, experts, and families who believe that Medicine belongs to the people.

By organizing as a cooperative, they ensure that 80 cents of every dollar goes back to the farmer. This “Farm-to-Table” model eliminates brokers, middle-men, and corporate stockholders, allowing us to bring you the highest quality organic CBD at the most honest price point possible.

The Legend of the “Fat Pig”

The name was born in Micronesia. To prove a new sustainable feeding method to skeptical local farmers, founder Bill Althouse raised one pig his way and one the traditional way. Six months later, the farmer’s pig was 75lbs—Bill’s was over 250lbs. The farmer looked at the results and said, “Wow, that’s a fat pig.” Today, the Coop uses that same “Demonstration Model” to prove that organic, vertical integration creates better medicine and better lives for farmers.

The $100K Strategy

Agricultural economists at CSU identified $100,000 as the “Magic Number” to save a family farm. The Coop’s goal is to ensure every member farmer hits this benchmark, keeping land out of the hands of developers and in the hands of the next generation.

  • ✔ 100% USDA Certified Organic
  • ✔ Vertically Integrated (Clones to Retail)
  • ✔ Regenerative Soil Practices
  • ✔ Worker-Owned & Community Funded

Bill Althouse

Founding Member & Innovator

One of the world’s first CBD producers, Bill pioneered USDA organic certifications for hemp. An energy engineer by trade, he has famously turned away venture capitalists with “suitcases of money” to ensure his technology only ever serves the small organic farmer.

Iginia Boccalandro

Founding Member & Visionary

A two-time Olympian and Certified Permaculture Designer, Iginia is the heart of the Coop. She relocated to Colorado in 2015 to help establish the Society, combining her passion for alternative medicine with a commitment to sustainable, circular economies.

pages.title: Privacy Policy
pages.content:
Effective Date: January 1st, 2025
Website: VillageSocialite.com

1. Who We Are

Village Socialite is a digital media platform focused on content, videos, and resources related to The Villages, Florida.

Our website address is: https://villagesocialite.com

2. Information We Collect

We collect information in the following ways:

a. Information You Provide

When you interact with the website, including:

  • Submitting contact forms
  • Leaving comments
  • Signing up or registering (if applicable)

We may collect personal information such as your name, email address, and any additional details you choose to provide.

b. Automatically Collected Information

We may collect certain technical data automatically, including:

  • IP address
  • Browser type and device information
  • Pages visited and interaction data

This information is used for security, analytics, and improving the user experience.

3. Comments

When visitors leave comments on the site, we collect:

  • Data shown in the comments form
  • Visitor IP address
  • Browser user agent string

This information is used to help detect spam and maintain site integrity.

An anonymized string (hash) of your email address may be shared with the Gravatar service to determine if you are using it.
Gravatar’s privacy policy is available here: https://automattic.com/privacy/

Once approved, your profile image may be publicly visible alongside your comment.

4. Media

If you upload images to the website, you should avoid uploading images with embedded location data (EXIF GPS), as visitors may be able to download and extract that information.

5. Cookies

We use cookies to enhance your experience on the site.

a. Comment Cookies

If you leave a comment, you may opt to save your name, email, and website in cookies for convenience. These cookies last up to one year.

b. Login Cookies

When visiting the login page, a temporary cookie is set to determine if your browser accepts cookies. This contains no personal data and is discarded upon closing your browser.

When logging in:

  • Login cookies last for two days
  • “Remember Me” extends login to two weeks
  • Screen preference cookies may last up to one year

c. Editing Cookies

If you edit or publish content, a temporary cookie may be stored indicating the post ID. This expires after one day.

6. Embedded Content & Third-Party Services

This website may include embedded content such as videos, images, or articles from third-party platforms (including YouTube).

Embedded content behaves as if you visited the third-party website directly.

These third-party services may:

  • Collect data about you
  • Use cookies
  • Track your interactions
  • Monitor engagement if you are logged into their platform

Village Socialite does not control these third-party practices.

7. Affiliate Links & External Services

Some links on this website may be affiliate links or lead to third-party services.

If you interact with these links:

  • Third-party platforms may collect data according to their own privacy policies
  • We may receive a commission or referral benefit

We are not responsible for the privacy practices of external websites.

8. How We Use Your Information

We use collected data to:

  • Operate and maintain the website
  • Respond to inquiries and communications
  • Improve user experience and content relevance
  • Monitor performance, security, and usage patterns

If you voluntarily provide contact information, we may contact you regarding your inquiry, updates, or relevant content.

We do not sell your personal information to third-party marketers.

9. Data Sharing

We may share limited information:

  • With trusted service providers (such as hosting, analytics, or spam detection tools)
  • With affiliated brands or partners when relevant to your request or inquiry

All such sharing is limited to what is necessary for functionality and service delivery.

10. Data Retention

  • Comments and related metadata may be stored indefinitely
  • Registered user information (if applicable) is stored until modified or deleted

This allows us to recognize and approve follow-up interactions efficiently.

11. Your Rights

If you have an account or have submitted data, you may request:

  • A copy of the personal data we hold about you
  • Deletion of your personal data

Please note that we may retain certain information as required for legal, administrative, or security purposes.

12. Security

We take reasonable measures to protect your information, but no method of transmission over the internet is completely secure.

Use of the website is at your own discretion and risk.

13. Where Your Data Is Processed

Visitor comments and interactions may be processed through automated spam detection services.

14. Changes to This Policy

We reserve the right to update or modify this Privacy Policy at any time.

Updates will be posted on this page with a revised effective date.

15. Contact

For questions regarding this Privacy Policy, please contact:
Website: https://villagesocialite.com/contact-village-socialite/

By using this website, you acknowledge and agree to the terms of this Privacy Policy.
